ontology_hints: Tool wear is a FailureEvent that occurs on a Feature (the tool) within a Platform (the machine).
It is a TemporalEntity, representing a time‑dependent failure event.
The spatial extent of the wear is a SpatialObject located on the Feature.
An Operator observes and records the event.
Tool wear triggers an Alarm and may require Intervention such as tool replacement or adjustment of spindle speed/feed rate.
relation_candidates.definition: Tool wear is the gradual failure of cutting tools during machining, manifested as flank, crater, notch, or built‑up edge wear. It is a time‑dependent degradation state that triggers an alarm when the tool’s performance deteriorates.

relation_candidates.definition: A power supply is an electrical device that supplies electric power to an electrical load. Its main purpose is to convert electric current from a source to the correct voltage, current, and frequency to power the load. Power supplies may be standalone or integrated into appliances, and they often include functions such as current limiting, fault shutdown, power conditioning, power‑factor correction, and energy storage for uninterruptible operation.
